title: Latex Beads Migration Assay
author: Jorge Fernandez Mendez
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8hwht7e

steps.title: Prepare two FF170HP strips and three...
steps.instruction: Prepare two FF170HP strips and three FF80HP strips of 1cm wide x 4 cm long. Wax print the microfluidic membranes following the protocol mentioned in the guideliness section.

steps.title: Block one FF170HP and two FF80HP...
steps.instruction: Block one FF170HP and two FF80HP membranes by immersion in 0.2 % BSA solution in PBS. Let the membranes dry for 1h at room temperature, and let them on a dissecator at 4ºC overnight.

steps.title: Aspire 20 µL of conjugated latex...
steps.instruction: Aspire 20 µL of conjugated latex beads stock, and pipette them on the sample deposition area of the sealed membranes. It's recommendable placing the strips with a briefly inclination degree, avoiding the sample to fall down trough the membrane surface. (Placing the sample deposition region on the down region).

steps.title: Wait 15 minutes until the liquid...
steps.instruction: Wait 15 minutes until the liquid in the sample has migrated completely. Results can be directly visualized. Wait until the membranes have dried to manipulate them.
